review_title: Head Tension and Tingling Sensation (my personal side effects)
review_text: The first thing I noticed was my bright yellow urine, which is pretty normal when taking B vitamins especially excess riboflavin. Which I didn’t pay too much attention and didn’t even bother to think why it occurred and though it will just subside, that being said I still continued to consume it daily. However, the tingling sensation persisted in addition of weird tension still in my right temple. I was flabbergasted, ironically the tingling sensation and tension would gradually start an hour or two after taking this certain supplement. So I suspected, this could be the culprit. Just three days ago, I stopped consuming this supplement, and since then to this day the tingling and tension didn’t come back. So, I guess my hypothesis was right. This my first time using this brand for my B vitamins supplement. I’m also currently taking Vitamin D3, K2 MK7, Zinc,Fish Oil and Magnesium on a daily basis yet I didn’t get any weird side effects aa mentioned above. Am I the only one having this? Lmk.

Read the following and you'll be the judge: B6 neuropathy, or peripheral neuropathy, is nerve damage in the hands and feet (tingling, numbness, pain) caused by excessive intake of vitamin B6 (pyridoxine) supplements, even at doses below 100mg/day, though high doses are more common culprits, and it's often found in multivitamins. Symptoms can worsen with continued use, potentially leading to balance issues and difficulty walking, so checking supplement labels and consulting a doctor is crucial before taking high-dose B6.

review_title: Handle with care - Affected me badly
review_text: I purchased this B Complex product, took it for 3 months. I ended up with numb hands at night, and sometimes while sitting and watching movies or tv. I read about many people in Australia having health problems and poisoning themselves after taking high levels of Vitamin B6. Google search: "Vitamin B6 toxicity is caused by taking excessive amounts of B6-containing supplements. This can cause peripheral neuropathy. The symptoms of peripheral neuropathy include numbness, tingling, pain, and weakness in the hands and feet." When I checked the % daily value of B6 in this product, it is 5,882% of RDA! It also has high levels of B12 12,500%; B5 10,000%;1 8,333%; B2 5,769%; B7 3,333%. I have stopped taking these capsules, and I don't have numb fingers anymore. Handle with Care! Be extremely careful!

B vitamins are generally safe because they are water soluble and your body excretes what it doesn’t need through urine. Toxicity is rare but possible with high doses. Some people experience nausea or vomiting, headaches, insomnia, skin rashes, flushing, fatigue or weakness. Long term excessive amounts can cause liver & nerve damage, irregular heartbeats or heart palpitations, high blood pressure, or serious allergic reactions. I started getting headaches, then was nauseous all the time. I was already taking this supplement with food. I tried taking one pill with or just after breakfast and the other pill with or just after dinner—I was still nauseous all the time. I finally thought to look at how much of each B vitamin was in this supplement compared to the recommended daily dose. Every B vitamin in this supplement is very excessive compared to the recommended daily dose. More is not always better—definitely not when it comes to your health!

review_title: Niacin (B3) Toxicity - Red Hot flushed face like others
review_text: Like many others, I had extreme flushing and burning of my skin around my face and ear’s especially. After it happened I researched and discovered that this product has too much B3 (Niacin). The proper daily dose of B3 should be 14mg for women and 17 mg for men. The Maximum safe dosage of Niacin is no more then 50mg. This product has over 6 times the RDA of Niacin, and twice the Maximum safe dose. Signs of Niacin toxicity include skin flushing, rapid heartbeat, itching, nausea and v*miting, abdominal pain, and d*arehha. I felt like my skin was on fire when I took this. Long term use can lead to liver damage.

review_title: Is this really #1 in the B-complex category?
review_text: As my title states, I am very surprised that this is #1 among the B-complex supplements. To get right to it, this was very disappointing supplement for me. With it being #1 and the bio-active ingredients, (Methyl B12 and Folate, which are actually a bit rare on b-complex formulas) I was really expecting something better. First, it didn't take it long to throw me on my tail. It made me extremely tired each time I took it. I think that has to do with the amount of each vitamin, but even while only taking 1 pill per day (the label recommends 2), it still made my heart beat quickly and made me tired. Another reason was because of the smell. While it started out smelling like a typical b-complex mix, after only about a week it started smelling terrible. It started smelling rotten, and I actually started to wonder if it was OK to continue taking. The third reason for the low score is it left a metallic taste in my mouth; one that I haven't had with other b-complexes. Now I am currently taking the Jarrow Formula B-Right b-complex, and that's actually going really well. While I realize that Life Extension is a well known company, this B-complex formula just didn't make the cut for me. Hopefully it wasn't as bad for others. After the first negative reactions to it, I definitely won't be ordering anymore.

review_title: My neuropathy increased due to excess vitamin B6
review_text: I have peripheral neuropathy so I must take vitamin B supplements for proper nerve function. The fact is that I took a little more than one bottle and felt a lot of pain and numbness in my feet and hands. I also experienced difficulty falling asleep. I started researching and found that excess vitamin B6 causes neuropathy or aggravates it when it is in the form of pyridoxine hydrochloride. In its P5P form it is harmless, what happens is that this supplement has high amounts of that vitamin in its 2 forms but it does not specify what amounts it has of each one. To conclude, this supplement did not work for me, but I still buy other supplements this brand that seems good to me despite everything.

Just because something is water-soluble does not necessarily mean that "it is safe to take a lot." For example, if you take a lot of vitamin B2 (riboflavin) at one time, absorption will plateau, and it will not be absorbed at a single dose of more than 27 mg. Vitamin B6 is absorbed after its phosphorylated form is broken down in the small intestine (jejunum), and is mainly absorbed by passive diffusion. High doses over a long period of time are known to pose a risk of peripheral neuropathy. The Australian TGA has published a decision stating that "peripheral neuropathy may occur in some people even at 50 mg/day or less." The European EFSA has also set the upper limit for adults at 12 mg/day. Vitamin B6: 100 mg / 2 capsules - as a doctor...

I've bought this product at least twice and was planning to continue. However, today (2025.0904), I ran an AI analysis and concluded the following: The content of multiple B vitamins in this product far exceeds the daily value (DV) of our diet, reaching therapeutic-grade, ultra-high doses. It's not suitable for daily health care, but rather for specific health needs (such as severe B vitamin deficiency, energy metabolism support, etc.) and should only be used under the guidance of a doctor or nutritionist. Does it exceed the DV? Unequivocally, yes. The doses of many ingredients are dozens or even hundreds of times the DV. Q: Can I take it? A: Exercise caution! If you haven't been examined by a doctor and have been specifically advised to take such high doses of B vitamins, do not purchase or take this product. For the vast majority of ordinary people looking to supplement their diet, they should choose a multivitamin or B vitamin product with a dose close to 100% DV. If you are taking any other medications or have a health condition, it's crucial to consult a doctor before use, as high doses of B vitamins may interact with certain medications. In short: This product is a "strong medicine," not a "health supplement." Avoid blind use. Therefore, it is not recommended for daily use. Everyone should be aware of the consequences of excessive supplementation: Vitamin B6 may cause severe peripheral neuritis, resulting in symptoms such as unsteady gait and numbness in the hands and feet; it may also cause allergic reactions such as rashes and itching. Vitamin B1 overdose is relatively rare and generally has low toxicity. However, long-term, excessive intake (such as far exceeding the recommended amount shown in the table) may cause headaches, fatigue, irritability, dizziness, swelling, and cardiac arrhythmias.

Most vitamin B complex medications contain three or more types of B vitamins, which include: Vitamin B1 (thiamine): Helps the body convert carbohydrates into energy and maintain the health of the brain, muscles, and nervous system. Vitamin B2 (riboflavin): Converts food into energy, acts as an antioxidant, and helps produce red blood cells. Vitamin B3 (Niacin): It helps in cell metabolism and DNA synthesis, and also plays an important role in the cell signaling process. Vitamin B5 (pantothenic acid): Contributes to energy production and the manufacture of cholesterol and hormones. Vitamin B6 (pyridoxine): Helps metabolize proteins, amino acids, produce red blood cells and maintain nerve health. Vitamin B7 (Biotin): It helps in the process of communication between cells and organizing genetic material. It is one of the most famous vitamins used to strengthen hair and nails. Vitamin B9 (folic acid): It plays an important role in the manufacture of amino acids and nucleic acids. It is also used to prevent fetal malformations during pregnancy and maintain the health of the heart and immune system. Vitamin B12 (cobalamin): It is considered the most famous of the B vitamins. It helps in the manufacture of DNA and red blood cells, and also maintains nerve functions in the body.
